{
  "title": "Invoice totals wrong when line-item quantity is updated concurrently",
  "body": "Steps to reproduce: open the same invoice in two tabs; tab A sets line item 1 qty=5; tab B sets line item 2 qty=3 before A saves; save both ~simultaneously. Expected: both changes persist, total = sum of all line items. Actual: one update silently overwrites the other; total shows only one change. Suspect: PATCH /invoices/:id recalculates total from the request body alone rather than re-reading all persisted line items. No optimistic-lock or version field. Environment: production, ~12 Team-plan users.",
  "labels": ["bug", "data-integrity", "priority-high"],
  "state": "open",
  "url": "https://github.com/acme/invoicely-saas/issues/214",
  "author": "freelancer-pat"

  "body": "GET /api/trips/:id/presence calls Redis with no timeout and no fallback. Expected: return an empty presence list with a Retry-After header, or at most a 503 with a sanitized error message. Classify this issue JSON (from `gh issue view`):
{
  "title": "Feed download counter double-counts requests that include Range header (podcast app resumable downloads)",
  "body": "Podcast apps (Apple Podcasts, Overcast) send HTTP Range requests to resume interrupted downloads or to fetch the first few bytes for duration probing. Our download counter increments once per HTTP request, not once per full-file download, so a single listener resuming a download registers as 2-4 downloads. Expected: one download per unique listener per episode per calendar day (dedup by subscriber_hash). Actual: every HTTP request including partial Range requests increments the counter. Dashboard shows 3-5x inflated download numbers. RFC 3005 compliance requires counting only full downloads or initial-range requests.",
  "labels": ["bug", "analytics", "rss", "priority:high"],
  "state": "open",
  "url": "https://github.com/acme/tapecast-feed/issues/482",
  "author": "datadog-oncall"
}

user:
Classify the following GitHub issue into one of: bug | feature | enhancement | refactor | chore | documentation. Emit structured JSON with issue_type, title, and reasoning. This gates routing: bug routes to archon-investigate-issue; all other types route to archon-create-plan.

Issue JSON:
{
  "number": 482,
  "title": "Delta sync endpoint returns duplicate records when client clock is skewed, causing mobile app data corruption",
  "state": "open",
  "url": "https://github.com/acme/academytrack-lms-mobile/issues/482",
  "author": "mobile-qa",
  "labels": ["bug", "sync", "mobile", "data-integrity", "priority:high"],
  "body": "**Describe the bug**\nEmployees using devices with a clock drift of more than ~1 second occasionally see course progress reset to zero on their mobile app after a sync. Investigation shows the delta sync endpoint returns the same lesson_progress rows multiple times across consecutive syncs, and the mobile SQLite merge logic treats a duplicate upsert of an older record as authoritative, overwriting newer local progress.\n\n**Root cause**\nGET /sync?since=<timestamp> uses a strict greater-than comparison (updatedAt > :since) with the client-supplied timestamp. Two problems: 1) the since parameter comes from the client device clock, which may be behind the server clock; 2) records updated at exactly since are excluded (off-by-one).\n\n**Expected**: Delta sync is monotonically consistent regardless of client clock skew.\n**Actual**: Records are duplicated or missed, corrupting local state.\n\n**Suggested fix**: Return a server-generated nextSyncToken (opaque cursor based on server-side MAX(updatedAt) at query time).\n\n**Affected versions**: all current production builds (iOS 2.3.1, Android 2.3.0)"
}

Classify this gh issue view JSON. Its output.issue_type gates Phase 3 routing (bug -> archon-investigate-issue; all others -> archon-create-plan).

{"title":"Tickets can be scanned successfully after event end time","body":"Gate staff report tickets for last night's show were accepted by the scanner up to 4 hours post-doors-close. The time validity check compares event.doors_close against the server clock, but the PWA caches the event record in IndexedDB at door-open and never refreshes it. The HMAC expiry field in the QR payload uses the ticket purchase timestamp rather than the event end time. Repro: open scanner, go offline, scan 6h after end -> admitted. Expected: rejected EXPIRED. Actual: admitted due to stale cached event data.","labels":["bug","security","offline","pwa","time-validity"],"state":"open","url":"https://github.com/org/venueops-ticketing/issues/214","author":"gate-lead"}
gold: {"issue_type": "bug", "title": "Tickets admitted after event end time due to stale cached event and wrong HMAC expiry", "reasoning": "The issue reports a concrete defect with a deterministic reproduction and an Expected-vs-Actual mismatch: tickets are admitted hours after doors_close when they should be rejected EXPIRED. The language (accepted when they should be rejected, stale cached data, repro steps) plus the 'bug' and 'security' labels are classic bug indicators — broken behavior in existing functionality, not a net-new capability or maintenance task. Routes to archon-investigate-issue."}

{"title":"Webhook idempotency check throws UnhandledPromiseRejection when Vercel KV is unavailable","body":"When Vercel KV (Redis) is down or hitting rate limits, the webhook endpoint POST /api/webhooks/[provider] crashes the Node process with an unhandled promise rejection instead of falling back gracefully. The idempotency check does `const seen = await kv.get(idempotencyKey);` but there is no try/catch around the KV call. The error propagates and Next.js doesn't catch it. Expected: if KV is unavailable, log the error and process the webhook anyway.","labels":["bug","webhooks","reliability","priority:medium"],"state":"open","url":"https://github.com/podscout/podscout-analytics/issues/214","author":"dmoraine"}

{
  "title": "[Bug] Check-in endpoint accepts expired JWT and admits attendees after token expiry",
  "labels": ["bug", "security", "priority:critical"],
  "state": "open",
  "url": "https://github.com/org/eventpass-mobile-api/issues/214",
  "author": "door-ops-lead",
  "body": "POST /api/v1/events/:eventId/checkins does not enforce JWT expiry when the X-Offline-Mode: 1 header is present. This header was intended only for the batch sync route but was accidentally checked in the main auth middleware as a global bypass. Steps to reproduce: obtain a valid JWT, wait >15 min for expiry, call POST /checkins with X-Offline-Mode: 1, check-in succeeds with HTTP 200. Expected: 401 Unauthorized regardless of X-Offline-Mode. Root cause: src/middleware/auth.ts line 28 places the bypass before expiry verification."
}

Classify this issue JSON into one of: bug, feature, enhancement, refactor, chore, documentation. Emit the required JSON object only.

{"title":"Driver location updates cause thundering herd: all subscribers process every message","body":"fleet.driver.location is published at ~1 Hz per driver. With 200 active drivers that is 200 msg/s. We have 3 dispatcher-service replicas each subscribing to fleet.driver.location with NATS core (not JetStream queue groups). All 3 replicas process every message — 600 effective msg/s of redundant DB writes. Expected: processed exactly once across replicas using a NATS JetStream durable queue group consumer. Actual: the subscription in location_consumer.go uses nats.Subscribe (core NATS), not a JetStream consumer with a queue group. Messages are also not persisted and will be lost if all replicas restart simultaneously.","labels":["bug","nats","performance","data-loss-risk"],"state":"open","url":"https://github.com/org/fleet-dispatch/issues/142","author":"dispatch-oncall"}

Classify this issue JSON from `gh issue view`:
{"number":412,"title":"Class capacity check has a TOCTOU race — double-bookings occur when two members book the last spot simultaneously","body":"Steps to reproduce: 1) class capacity=1, 0 bookings; 2) two simultaneous POST /api/bookings for two members; 3) both succeed, bookings table has 2 rows. Root cause: bookClass() reads count, compares to capacity, then inserts as separate statements with no locking. The advisory lock path exists but the booking API route never passes useAdvisoryLock:true.","labels":["bug","concurrency","data-integrity","priority:high"],"state":"open","url":"https://github.com/org/gympass-access/issues/412","author":"mreyes"}
gold: {"issue_type": "bug", "title": "TOCTOU race in class capacity check causes double-bookings", "reasoning": "The issue reports broken behavior with concrete reproduction steps where a capacity-1 class ends up with 2 confirmed bookings, and identifies a root cause (non-atomic count-then-insert with no locking) — classic regression/data-integrity signals. The 'bug', 'concurrency', and 'data-integrity' labels confirm it. There is no request to add a new capability (not feature/enhancement), no restructuring-without-behavior-change goal (not refactor), no dependency/maintenance work (not chore), and no docs target (not documentation)."}
